heading: Voluntary separation incentive payments
label: 4505 Voluntary separation incentive payments

children.heading: Authority to offer payments
children.label: a Authority to offer payments
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the head of any office in the legislative branch may establish a program under which voluntary separation incentive payments may be offered to eligible employees of the office to encourage such employees to separate from service voluntarily (whether by retirement or resignation), in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Amount and administration of payments
children.label: b Amount and administration of payments

children.text: shall be paid in a lump sum after the employee’s separation;

children.label: A
children.text: an amount equal to the amount the employee would be entitled to receive under , if the employee were entitled to payment under such section (without adjustment for any previous payment made); orsection 5595(c) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: an amount determined by the head of the office involved, not to exceed $25,000;
may be made only in the case of an employee who voluntarily separates (whether by retirement or resignation) under this section;
shall not be a basis for payment, and shall not be included in the computation, of any other type of Government benefit;
shall not be taken into account in determining the amount of any severance pay to which the employee may be entitled under based on any other separation; andsection 5595 of title 5
shall be paid from appropriations or funds available for the payment of the basic pay of the employee.

children.heading: Plan required for making payments
children.label: 1 Plan required for making payments
children.text: No voluntary separation incentive payment may be paid under this section with respect to an office unless the head of the office submits a plan described in paragraph (2) to each applicable committee described in paragraph (3), and each applicable committee approves the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: The specific positions and functions to be reduced or eliminated.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of which categories of employees will be offered incentives.

children.label: C
children.text: The time period during which incentives may be paid.

children.label: D
children.text: The number and amounts of voluntary separation incentive payments to be offered.

children.label: E
children.text: A description of how the office will operate without the eliminated positions and functions.

children.heading: Applicable committee
children.label: 3 Applicable committee
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the “applicable committee” with respect to an office means any committee of the House of Representatives or Senate with jurisdiction over the activities of the office under the applicable rules of the House of Representatives and the Senate (as determined by the head of the office), but does not include the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 112–74, div. G, title I, § 1401(a)(1)125 Stat. 1134Dec. 23, 2011
children.label: d Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 112–74, div. G, title I, § 1401(a)(1)125 Stat. 1134Dec. 23, 2011
A
children.text: is serving under an appointment without time limitation; and

children.label: B
children.text: has been currently employed for a continuous period of at least 3 years.

children.label: A
children.text: A reemployed annuitant under subchapter III of chapter 83 or 84 of title 5 or another retirement system for employees of the Government.

children.label: B
children.text: An employee having a disability on the basis of which such employee is or would be eligible for disability retirement under subchapter III of chapter 83 or 84 of title 5 or another retirement system for employees of the Government.

children.label: C
children.text: An employee who is in receipt of a decision notice of involuntary separation for misconduct or unacceptable performance.

children.label: D
children.text: An employee who has previously received any voluntary separation incentive payment from the Federal Government under this section or any other authority.

children.label: E
children.text: An employee covered by statutory reemployment rights who is on transfer employment with another organization.

children.label: i
children.text: during the 36-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a student loan repayment benefit was or is to be paid under or any other authority;section 5379 of title 5

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 24-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a recruitment or relocation bonus was or is to be paid under section 5753 of such title or any other authority; or

children.label: iii
children.text: during the 12-month period preceding the date of separation of that employee, performed service for which a retention bonus was or is to be paid under section 5754 of such title or any other authority.

children.heading: Repayment for individuals returning to Government employment
children.label: f Repayment for individuals returning to Government employment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), an employee who has received a voluntary separation incentive payment under this section and accepts employment with the Government of the United States within 5 years after the date of the separation on which the payment is based shall be required to repay the entire amount of the incentive payment to the office that paid the incentive payment.

children.heading: Waiver for individuals possessing unique abilities
children.label: 2 Waiver for individuals possessing unique abilities
A
children.text: If the employment is with an Executive agency (as defined by , but excluding the Government Accountability Office), the Director of the Office of Personnel Management may, at the request of the head of the agency, waive the repayment required under this subsection if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.section 105 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: If the employment is with an entity in the legislative branch, the head of the entity or the appointing official may waive the repayment required under this subsection if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.

children.label: C
children.text: If the employment is with the judicial branch, the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may waive the repayment required under this subsection if the individual involved possesses unique abilities and is the only qualified applicant available for the position.

children.heading: Treatment of personal services contracts
children.label: 3 Treatment of personal services contracts
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1) (but not paragraph (2)), the term “employment” includes employment under a personal services contract with the United States.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: g Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect on , and shall apply with respect to the portion of fiscal year 2005 occurring on and after , and to each succeeding fiscal year.December 8, 2004December 8, 2004
